classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XL

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of all those things which our Lord Jesus Christ has foretold, we know part to have been already accomplished, part we hope will be accomplished hereafter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All of them, however, will be fulfilled, because He is “the Truth” who speaks them, and requires of us to be as “faithful,” as He Himself speaks them faithfully.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us say then what this Psalm says.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I waited patiently for the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I waited patiently for the promise of no mere mortal who can both deceive and be himself deceived: I waited for the consolation of no mere mortal, who may be consumed by sorrow of his own, before he gives me comfort.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Should a brother mortal attempt to comfort me, when he himself is in sorrow likewise?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us mourn in company; let us weep together, let us “wait patiently” together, let us join our prayers together also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom did I wait for but for the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord, who though He puts off the fulfilment of His promises, yet never recalls them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will make it good; assuredly He will make it good, because He has made many of His promises good already: and of God’s truth we ought to have no fears, even if as yet He had made none of them good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo! let us henceforth think thus, “He has promised us everything; He has not as yet given us possession of anything; He is a sponsible Promiser; a faithful Paymaster: do you but show yourself a dutiful exactor of what is promised; and if you be “weak,” if you be one of the little ones, claim the promise of His mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do you not see tender lambs striking their dams’ teats with their heads, in order that they may get their fill of milk?…“And He took heed unto me, and heard my cry.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He took heed to it, and He heard it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See thou hast not waited in vain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His eyes are over thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His ears turned towards thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “the eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous, and His ears are open unto their cry.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did He not see thee, when thou usedst to do evil and to blaspheme Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then becomes of what is said in that very Psalm, “The face of the Lord is upon them that do evil”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But for what end? “that He may cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, even when thou wert wicked, He “took heed of thee;” but He “took no heed to thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then to him who “waited patiently for the Lord,” it was not enough to say, “He took heed of me, He says, “He took heed to me;” that is, He took heed by comforting me, that He might do me good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was it that He took heed to? “and He heard my cry.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what hath He accomplished for thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath He done for thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He brought me up also out of a horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath given us great blessings already: and still He is our debtor; but let him who hath this part of the debt repaid already, believe that the rest will be also, seeing that he ought to have believed even before he received anything.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord has employed facts themselves to persuade us, that He is a faithful promiser, a liberal giver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then has He already done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He has brought me out of a horrible pit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What horrible pit is that?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the depth of iniquity, from the lusts of the flesh, for this is meant by “the miry clay.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence hath He brought thee out?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of a certain deep, out of which thou criedst out in another Psalm, “Out of the deep have I called unto Thee, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And those who are already “crying out of the deep,” are not absolutely in the lowest deep: the very act of crying is already lifting them up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are some deeper in the deep, who do not even perceive themselves to be in the deep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such are those who are proud despisers, not pious entreaters for pardon; not tearful criers for mercy: but such as Scripture thus describes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The sinner when he comes into the depth of evil despiseth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he is deeper in the deep, who is not satisfied with being a sinner, unless instead of confessing he even defends his sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he who has already “cried out of the deep,” hath already lifted up his head in order that he might “cry out of the deep,” has been heard already, and has been “brought out of the horrible pit, and out of the mire and clay.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He already has faith, which he had not before; he has hope, which he was before without; he now walks in Christ, who before used to go astray in the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For on that account it is that he says, “He hath set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now “that Rock was Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Supposing that we are “upon the rock,” and that our “goings are ordered,” still it is necessary that we continue to walk; that we advance to something farther.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what did the Apostle Paul say when now upon the Rock, when his “goings had now been established”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Not as though I had already attained, either were already perfect: Brethren, I count not myself to have apprehended.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then has been done for thee, if thou hast not apprehended?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On what account dost thou return thanks, saying, “But I have obtained mercy”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because his goings are now established, because he now walks on the Rock?…Therefore, when he was saying, “I press forward toward the prize of my high calling,” because “his feet were now set on the Rock,” and “his goings were ordered,” because he was now walking on the right way, he had something to return thanks for; something to ask for still; returning thanks for what he had received already, while he was claiming that which still remained due.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what things already received was he giving thanks?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the remission of sins, for the illumination of faith; for the strong support of hope, for the fire of charity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in what respects had he still a claim of debt on the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Henceforth,” he says, “there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is therefore something due me still.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it that is due?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, shall give me at that day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was at first a loving Father to “bring him forth from the horrible pit;” to forgive his sins, to rescue him from “the mire and clay;” hereafter he will be a “righteous Judge,” requiting to him walking rightly, what He promised; to him (I say), unto whom He had at the first granted that power to walk rightly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then as a “righteous Judge” will repay; but whom will he repay?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He that endureth unto the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He hath put a new song in my mouth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What new song is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even a hymn unto our God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps you used to sing hymns to strange gods; old hymns, because they were uttered by the “old man,” not by the “new man;” let the “new man” be formed, and let him sing a “new song;” being himself made “new,” let him love those “new” things by which he is himself made new.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is more Ancient than God, who is before all things, and is without end and without beginning?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He becomes “new” to thee, when thou returnest to Him; because it was by departing from Him, that thou hadst become old; and hadst said, “I have waxed old because of all mine enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We therefore utter “a hymn unto our God;” and the hymn itself sets us free.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I will call upon the Lord to praise Him, and I will be safe from all mine enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a hymn is a song of praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Call on God to “praise” Him, not to find fault with Him.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If haply any one asks, what person is speaking in this Psalm?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I would say briefly, “It is Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as ye know, brethren, and as we must say frequently, Christ sometimes speaks in His own Person, in the Person of our Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He Himself is “the Saviour of the Body.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is our Head; the Son of God, who was born of the Virgin, suffered for us, “rose again for our justification,” sitteth “at the right hand of God,” to “make intercession for us:” who is also to recompense to the evil and to the good, in the judgment, all the evil and the good that they have done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He deigned to be come our Head; to become “the Head of the Body,” by taking of us that flesh in which He should die for us; that flesh which He also raised up again for our sakes, that in that flesh He might place before us an instance of the resurrection; that we might learn to hope for that of which we heretofore despaired, and might henceforth have our feet upon the rock, and might walk in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then sometimes speaks in the name of our Head; sometimes also He speaks of us who are His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For both when He said, “I was an hungred, and ye gave Me meat,” He spoke on behalf of His members, not of Himself: and when He said, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou Me?” the Head was crying on behalf of its members: and yet He did not say, “Why dost thou persecute My members?” but, “Why persecutest thou Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He suffers in us, then shall we also be crowned in Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such is the love of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is there can be compared to this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the thing on account of which “He hath put a hymn in our mouth,” and this He speaks on behalf of His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The just shall see, and shall fear, and shall trust in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The just shall see.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the just?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The faithful; because it is “by faith that the just shall live.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is in the Church this order, some go before, others follow; and those who go before make themselves “an example” to those who follow; and those who follow imitate those who go before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But do those then follow no one, who exhibit themselves as an ensample to them that come after?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they follow no one at all, they will fall into error.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These persons then must themselves also follow some one, that is, Christ Himself.…“The just,” therefore, “shall see, and shall fear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They see a narrow way on the one hand; on the other side, “a broad road:” on this side they see few, on the other many.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou art a just man; count them not, but weigh them; bring “a just balance,” not a “deceitful” one: because thou art called just.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The just shall see, and fear,” applies to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Count not therefore the multitudes of men that are filling the “broad ways,” that are to fill the circus tomorrow; celebrating with shouts the City’s Anniversary, while they defile the City itself by evil living.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look not at them; they are many in number; and who can count them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are a few travelling along the narrow road.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bring forth the balance, I say.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Weigh them; see what a quantity of chaff you lift up on the one side, against a few grains of corn on the other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let this be done by “the just,” the “believers,” who are to follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what shall they who precede do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them not be proud, let them not “exalt themselves;” let them not deceive those who follow them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How may they deceive those who follow them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By promising them salvation in themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then ought those who follow to do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The just shall see, and fear: and shall trust in the Lord;” not in those who go before them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But indeed they fix their eyes on those who go before them, and follow and imitate them; but they do so, because they consider from Whom they have received the grace to go before them; and because they trust in Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although therefore they make these their models, they place their trust in Him from whom the others have received the grace whereby they are such as they are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The just shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as in another Psalm, “I lift up mine eyes unto the hills,” we understand by hills, all distinguished and great spiritual persons in the Church; great in solidity, not by swollen inflation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By these it is that all Scripture hath been dispensed unto us; they are the Prophets, they are the Evangelists; they are sound Doctors: to these “I lift up mine eyes, from whence shall come my help.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And lest you should think of mere human help, he goes on to say, “My help cometh from the Lord, which made heaven and earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The just shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the Lord.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed is that man that maketh the name of the Lord his trust, and hath not respected vanities or lying madnesses” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the way by which thou wouldest fain have gone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the “multitude that fill the Broad way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not without reason “that” road leads to the amphitheatre.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not without reason it leads to Death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “broad way” leads unto death, its breadth delights for time: its end is straitness to all eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Aye; but the multitudes murmur; the multitudes are rejoicing together; the multitudes are hastening along; the multitudes are flocking together!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not thou imitate them; do not turn aside after them: they are “vanities, and lying madnesses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Lord thy God be thy hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hope for nothing else from the Lord thy God; but let the Lord thy God Himself be thine hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many persons hope to obtain from God’s hands riches, and many perishable and transitory honours; and, in short, anything else they hope to obtain at God’s hands, except only God Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But do thou seek after thy God Himself: nay, indeed, despising all things else, make thy way unto Him!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Forget other things, remember Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Leave other things behind, and “press forward” unto Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely it is He Himself, who set thee right, when turned away from the right path; who, now that thou art set in the right path, guides thee aright, who guides thee to thy destination.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him then be thy hope, who both guides thee, and guides thee to thy destination.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whither does worldly covetousness lead thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And to what point does it conduct thee at the last?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst at first desire a farm; then thou wouldest possess an estate; thou wouldest shut out thy neighbours; having shut them out, thou didst set thy heart on the possessions of other neighbours; and didst extend thy covetous desires till thou hadst reached the shore: arriving at the shore, thou covetest the islands: having made the earth thine own, thou wouldest haply seize upon heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Leave thou all thy loves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who made heaven and earth is more beautiful than all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed is the man that maketh the name of the Lord his hope, and who hath not regarded vanities and lying madnesses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whence is it that “madness” is called “lying”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Insanity is a lying thing, even as it is sanity that sees the Truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what thou seest as good things, thou art deceived; thou art not in thy sound senses: a violent fever has driven thee to frenzy: that which thou art in love with is not a reality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou applaudest the charioteer; thou cheerest the charioteer; thou art madly in love with the charioteer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is “vanity;” it is “a lying madness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It is ‘not’” (he cries).

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nothing can be better; nothing more delightful.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What can I do for one in a state of high fever?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pray ye for such persons, if you have any feelings of compassion in you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the physician himself also in a desperate case generally turns to those in the house, who stand around weeping; who are hanging on his lips to hear his opinion of the patient who is sick and in danger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The physician stands in a state of doubt: he sees not any good to promise; he fears to pronounce evil, lest he should excite alarm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He devises a thoroughly modest sentence: “The good God can do all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pray ye for him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which then of these madmen shall I check?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which of them will listen to me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which of them would not call us miserable?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they suppose us to have lost great and various pleasures, of which they are madly fond, in that we are not as madly in love with them as they are: and they do not see that they are “lying” pleasures.…“And hath not respected vanities, and lying madnesses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Such a one has won,” he cries; “he harnessed such and such a horse,” he proclaims aloud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He would fain be a kind of diviner; he aspires to the honours of divination by abandoning the fountain of Divinity; and he frequently pronounces an opinion, and is frequently mistaken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even because they are “lying madnesses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why is it that what they say sometimes comes true?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That they may lead astray the foolish ones; that by loving the semblance of truth there, they may fall into the snare of falsehood: let them be left behind, let them be “given over,” let them be “cut off.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they were members of us, they must be mortified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mortify,” he says, “your members which are upon the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let our God be our hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who made all things, is better than all!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who made what is beautiful, is more beautiful than all that is such.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who made whatever is mighty, is Himself mightier.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who made whatever is great, is Himself greater.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will be unto you everything that you love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Learn in the creature to love the Creator; and in the work Him who made it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not that which has been made by Him detain thine affections, so that thou shouldest lose Him by whom thou thyself wert made also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed,” then, “is the man that maketh the Name of the Lord his trust, and hath not respected vanities and lying madnesses.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We will give him other sights in exchange for such sights as these.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what sights shall we present to the Christian, whom we would fain divert from those sights?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I thank the Lord our God; He in the following verse of the Psalm hath shown us what sights we ought to present and offer to spectators who would fain have sights to see?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us now suppose him to be weaned from the circus, the theatre, the amphitheatre; let him be looking after, let him by all means be looking after, some sight to see; we do not leave him without a spectacle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then shall we give in exchange for those?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what follows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Many, O Lord my God, are the wonderful works which Thou hast made” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He used to gaze at the “wonderful works” of man; let him now contemplate the wonderful works of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Many are the wonderful works” that God “has made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why are they become vile in his eyes?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He praises the charioteer guiding four horses; running all of them without fault and without stumbling.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps the Lord has not made such “wonderful works” in things spiritual.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him control lust, let him control cowardice, let him control injustice, let him control imprudence, I mean, the passions which falling into excess produce those vices; let him control these and bring them into subjection, and let him hold the reins, and not suffer himself to be carried away; let him guide them the way he himself would have them go; let him not be forced away whither he would not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He used to applaud the charioteer, he himself shall be applauded for his own charioteering; he used to call out that the charioteer should be invested with a dress of honour; he shall himself be clothed with immortality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the spectacles, these the sights that God exhibits to us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He cries out of heaven, “My eyes are upon you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Strive, and ‘I will’ assist you; triumph, and I will crown you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in Thy thought there is none that is like unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then look at the actor!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the man hath by dint of great pains learnt to walk upon a rope; and hanging there he holds thee hanging in suspense.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Turn to Him who exhibits spectacles far more wonderful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This man hath learned to walk upon the rope; but hath he caused another to walk on the sea?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Forget now thy theatre; behold our Peter; not a walker on the rope, but, so to speak, a walker on the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And do thou also walk on other waters (though not on those on which Peter walked, to symbolize a certain truth), for this world is a sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath a deleterious bitterness; it hath the waves of tribulations, the tempests of temptations; it hath men in it who, like fish, delight in their own ruin, and prey upon each other; walk thou here, set thou thy foot on this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wouldest see sights; be thyself a “spectacle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That thy spirit may not sink, look on Him who goes before thee, and says, “We have been made a spectacle unto this world, and unto angels, and unto men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Tread thou on the waters; suffer not thyself to be drowned in the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt not go there, thou wilt not “tread it under foot,” unless it be His bidding, who was Himself the first to walk upon the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was thus that Peter spoke.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If Thou art, bid me come unto Thee on the waters.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because “He was,” He heard him when praying; He granted his wish to him when expressing his desire; He raised him up when sinking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the “wonderful works” that the “Lord hath made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look on them; let faith be the eye of him who would behold them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And do thou also likewise; for although the winds alarm thee, though the waves rage against thee, and though human frailty may have inspired thee with some doubt of thy salvation, thou hast it in thy power to “cry out,” thou mayest say “Lord, I perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who bids thee walk there, suffers thee not to perish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in that thou now walkest “on the Rock,” thou fearest not even on the sea!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou art without “the Rock,” thou must sink in the sea; for the Rock on which thou must walk is such an one as is not sunk in the sea,

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe then the “wonderful works” of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have declared, and have spoken; they are multiplied beyond number.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is “a number,” there are some over and above the number.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a fixed number that belongs to that heavenly Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “the Lord knoweth them that are His;” the Christians that fear Him, the Christians that believe, the Christians that keep the commandments, that walk in God’s ways, that keep themselves from sins; that if they fall confess: they belong to “the number.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But are they the only ones?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are also some “beyond the number.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even if they be but a few (a few in comparison of the numbers of the larger majority), with how great numbers are our Churches filled, crowded up to the very walls; to what a degree do they annoy each other by the pressure, and almost choke each other by their overflowing numbers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, out of these very same persons, when there is a public spectacle, there are numbers flocking to the amphitheatre; these are over and above “the number.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is for this reason that we say this, that they may be in “the number.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not being present, they do not hear this from us; but when ye have gone from hence, let them hear it from you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have declared,” he says, “and have spoken.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is Christ who speaks.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath declared it,” in His own Person, as our Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath Himself declared it by His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He Himself hath sent those who should “declare” it; He Himself hath sent the Apostles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their sound is gone out into all lands, and their words unto the ends of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great the number of believers that are gathered together; how great the multitudes that flock together; many of them truly converted, many but in appearance: and those who are truly converted are the minority; those who are so but in appearance are the majority: because “they are multiplied beyond the number.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11. …These are the “wonderful works” of God; these are the “thoughts” of God, to which “no man’s thoughts are like;” that the lover of sight-seeing may be weaned from curiosity: and with us may seek after those more excellent, those more profitable things, in which, when he shall have attained unto them, he will rejoice.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sacrifice and offering Thou didst not desire” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6), saith the Psalm to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the men of old time, when as yet the true Sacrifice, which is known to the faithful, was foreshown in figures, used to celebrate rites that were figures of the reality that was to be hereafter; many of them understanding their meaning; but more of them in ignorance of it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Prophets and the holy Patriarchs understood what they were celebrating; but the rest of the “stiff-necked people” were so carnal, that what was done by them was but to symbolize the things that were to come afterwards; and it came to pass, when that first sacrifice was abolished; when the burnt-offerings of “rams, of goats, and of calves,” and of other victims, had been abolished, “God did not desire them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did God not desire them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And why did He at the first desire them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because all those things were, as it were, the words of a person making a promise; and the expressions conveying a promise, when the thing that they promise is come, are no longer uttered.…Those sacrifices then, as being but expressions of a promise, have been abrogated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that which has been given as its fulfilment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That “Body;” which ye know; which ye do not all of you know; which, of you who do know it, I pray God all may not know it unto condemnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe the time when it was said; for the person is Christ our Lord, speaking at one time for His members, at another in His own person.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sacrifice and offering,” said He, “Thou didst not desire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are we left at this present time without a sacrifice?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But a Body hast Thou perfected for me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was for this reason that Thou didst not desire the others; that Thou mightest “perfect” this; before Thou “perfectedst” this, Thou didst desire the others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The fulfilment of the promise has done away with the words that express the promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they still hold out a promise, that which was promised is not yet fulfilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was promised by certain signs; the signs that convey the promise are done away; because the Substance that was promised is come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are in this “Body.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are partakers of this “Body.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know that which we ourselves receive; and ye who know it not yet, will know it bye and bye; and when ye come to know it, I pray ye may not receive it unto condemnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation unto himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A Body” hath been “perfected” for us; let us be made perfect in the Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Burnt-offerings also for sin hast Thou not required.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then said I, Lo, I come!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is time that what “was promised should come;” because the signs, by means of which they were promised, have been put away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And indeed, Brethren, observe these put away; those fulfilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Jewish nation at this time show me their priest, if they can!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where are their sacrifices?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are brought to an end; they are put away now.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Should we at that time have rejected them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We do reject them now; because, if you chose to celebrate them now, it were unseasonable; unfitting at the time; incongruous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You are still making promises; I have already received!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There has remained to them a certain thing for them to celebrate; that they might not remain altogether without a sign.…In such a case then are they; like Cain with his mark.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sacrifices, however, which used to be performed there, have been put away; and that which remained unto them for a sign like that of Cain, hath by this time been fulfilled; and they know it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They slay the Lamb; they eat the unleavened bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Christ has been sacrificed for us, as our Passover.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, in the sacrifice of Christ, I recognise the Lamb that was slain!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What of the unleavened bread?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore,” says he, “let us keep the feast; not with old leaven, neither with the leaven of wickedness” (he shows what is meant by “old;” it is “stale” flour; it is sour), “but in the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have continued in the shade; they cannot abide the Sun of Glory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are already in the light of day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have “the Body” of Christ, we have the Blood of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we have a new life, let us “sing a new song, even a hymn unto our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Burnt offerings for sin Thou didst not desire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then said I, Lo, I come!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the head of the Book it is written of me, that I should fulfil Thy will: O my God, I am willing, and Thy Law is within my heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He turns His regards to His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath Himself “fulfilled the will” of the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in what “beginning of a Book” is it written of Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps in the beginning of this Book of Psalms.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For why should we seek far for it, or examine into other books for it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is written in the beginning of this Book of Psalms!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His will is in the Law of the Lord;” that is, “‘O my God, I am willing,’ and ‘Thy Law is within my heart;’” that is the same as, “And in His Law doth he meditate day and night.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have well declared Thy righteousness in the great congregation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He now addresses His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is exhorting them to do what He has already done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has “declared;” let us declare also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has suffered; let us “suffer with Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has been glorified; we shall be “glorified with Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have declared Thy righteousness in the great congregation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great an one is that?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In all the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great is it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even among all nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why among all nations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He is “the Seed of Abraham, in whom all nations shall be blessed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why among all nations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because their sound hath gone forth into all lands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will not refrain my lips, O Lord, and that Thou knowest.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My lips speak; I will not “refrain” them from speaking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My lips indeed sound audibly in the ears of men; but “Thou knowest” mine heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will not refrain my lips, O Lord; that Thou knowest.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is one thing that man heareth; another that God “knoweth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That the “declaring” of it should not be confined to the lips alone, and that it might not be said of us, “Whatsoever things they say unto you, do; but do not after their works;” or lest it should be said to the people, “praising God with their lips, but not with their heart,” “This people honoureth Me with their lips, but their heart is far from Me;” do thou make audible confession with thy lips; draw nigh with thine heart also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For with the heart man believeth unto righteousness; but with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In case like unto which that thief was found, who, hanging on the Cross with the Lord, did on the Cross acknowledge the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Others had refused to acknowledge Him while working miracles; this man acknowledged Him when hanging on the Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That thief had every other member pierced through; his hands were fastened by the nails; his feet were pierced also; his whole body was fastened to the tree; the body was not disengaged in its other members; the heart and the tongue were disengaged; “with the heart” he “believed; with the tongue” he made “confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember me, O Lord,” he said, “when Thou comest into Thy kingdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hoped for the coming of his salvation at a time far remote; he was content to receive it after a long delay; his hope rested on an object far remote.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The day, however, was not postponed!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The answer was, “This day shalt thou be with Me in Paradise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Paradise hath happy trees!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This day hast thou been with Me on “the Tree” of the Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This day shalt thou be with Me on “the Tree” of Salvation.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have not hid my righteousness within my heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by “my righteousness”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “the just shall live by faith.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As suppose the persecutor under threat of punishment, as they were once allowed to do, puts you to the question, “What art thou?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pagan or Christian?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A Christian.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is his “righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He believeth; he “lives by faith.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth not “hide his righteousness within his heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has not said in his heart, “I do indeed believe in Christ; but I will not tell what I believe to this persecutor, who is raging against me, and threatening me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My God knoweth that inwardly, within my heart, I do believe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knoweth that I renounce Him not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo! you say that you have this inwardly within your heart!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What have you upon your lips?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am not a Christian.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Your lips bear witness against your heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have not hid my righteousness within my heart.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have declared Thy Truth and Thy Salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have declared Thy Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the meaning of, “I have declared Thy Truth and Thy Salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is “Thy Truth” Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am the Truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is Christ “His Salvation”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Simeon recognised the infant in His Mother’s hands in the Temple, and said, “For mine eyes have seen Thy Salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The old man recognised the little child; the old man having himself “become a little child” in that infant, having been renewed by faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he had received an oracle from God; and it said this, “The Lord had said unto him, that he was not to depart out of this life, until he had seen the “Salvation of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This “Salvation of God” it is a good thing to have shown unto men; but let them cry, “Show us Thy mercy, O Lord, and grant us Thy Salvation.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have not concealed Thy mercy and Thy Truth from the great congregation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us be there; let us also be numbered among the members of this Body: let us not keep back “the mercy” of the Lord, and “the Truth” of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest thou hear what “the mercy of the Lord” is?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Depart from thy sins; He will forgive thy sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest thou hear what “the truth” of the Lord is?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hold fast righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy righteousness shall receive a crown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For mercy is announced to you now; “Truth” is to be shown unto thee hereafter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God is not merciful in such a way as not to be just, nor just in such a way as not to be merciful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Does that mercy seem to thee an inconsiderable one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will not impute unto thee all thy former sins: thou hast lived ill up to this present day; thou art still living; this day live well; then thou wilt not “conceal” this “mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If this is meant by “mercy,” what is meant by “truth”?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remove not Thou Thy mercies far from me, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is turning his attention to the wounded members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because I have not “concealed Thy mercy and Thy Truth from the great congregation,” from the Unity of the Universal Church, look Thou on Thy afflicted members, look on those who are guilty of sins of omission, and on those who are guilty of sins of commission: and withhold not Thou Thy mercies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy mercy and Thy Truth have continually preserved me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I should not dare to turn from my evil way, were I not assured of remission; I could not endure so as to persevere, if I were not assured of the fulfilment of Thy promise.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Innumerable evils have compassed me about” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can number sins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can count his own sins, and those of others?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A burden under which he was groaning, who said, “Cleanse Thou me from my secret faults; and from the faults of others, spare Thou Thy servant, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our own are too little; those “of others” are added to the burden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I fear for myself; I fear for a virtuous brother, I have to bear with a wicked brother; and under such burthen what shall we be, if God’s mercy were to fail?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thou, Lord, remove not afar off.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be Thou near unto us!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To whom is the Lord near?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even” unto them that “are of a broken heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is far from the proud: He is near to the humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For though the Lord is high, yet hath He respect unto the lowly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let not those that are proud think themselves to be unobserved: for the things that are high, He “beholdeth afar off.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He “beheld afar off” the Pharisee, who boasted himself; He was near at hand to succour the Publican, who made confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The one extolled his own merits, and concealed his wounds; the other boasted not of his merits, but laid bare his wounds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came to the Physician; he knew that he was sick, and that he required to be made whole; he “dared not lift up his eyes to Heaven: he smote upon his breast.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He spared not himself, that God might spare him; he acknowledged himself guilty, that God might “ignore” the charge against him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He punished himself, that God might free him from punishment.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I could not see.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a something for us “to see;” what prevents us so that we see it not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it not iniquity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From beholding this light your eye is prevented perhaps by some humour penetrating into it; perhaps by smoke, or dust, or by something else that has been thrown into it: and you have not been able to raise your wounded eye to contemplate this light of day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will you be able to lift up your wounded heart unto God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Must it not be first healed, in order that thou mayest see?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do you not show your pride, when you say, “First let me see, and then I will believe”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is there who says this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who that would fain see, says, “Let me see, and then I will believe”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am about to manifest the Light unto thee; or rather the Light Itself would fain manifest Itself to thee!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It cannot manifest Itself to the blind.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He does not see.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence is it that he seeth not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is that the eye is clogged by the multitude of sins.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They are more than the hairs of my head.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He subjects the number of the “hairs of his head” to calculation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is there can calculate the number of the hairs of his head?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Much less can he tell the number of his sins, which exceed the number of the hairs of his head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They seem to be minute; but they are many in number.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You have guarded against great ones; you do not now commit adultery, or murder; you do not plunder the property of others; you do not blaspheme; and do not bear false witness; those are the weightier kind of sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You have guarded against great sins, what are you doing about your smaller ones?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You have cast off the weight; beware lest the sand overwhelm you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my heart hath forsaken me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What wonder if thine heart is forsaken by thy God, when it is even “forsaken” by itself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by “faileth me,” “forsaketh me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not capable of knowing itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He means this: “My heart hath forsaken me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I would fain see God with mine heart, and cannot from the multitude of my sins: that is not enough; mine heart does not even know itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no one thoroughly knows himself: let no one presume upon his own state.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was Peter able to comprehend with his own heart the state of his own heart, who said, “I will be with Thee even unto death”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was a false presumption in the heart; there was lurking in that heart at the same time a real fear: and the heart was not able to comprehend the state of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Its state was unknown to the sick heart itself: it was manifest to the physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which was foretold of him was fulfilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God knew that in him which he knew not in himself: because his heart had forsaken him, his heart was unknown to his heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if he were saying, “‘If Thou wilt, Thou canst make me clean.’

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be pleased to deliver me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord, look upon me to help me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look, that is, on the penitent members, members that lie in pain, members that are writhing under the instruments of the surgeon; but still in hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be ashamed and confounded together that seek after my soul to destroy it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in a certain passage he makes an accusation, and says, “I looked upon my right hand, and beheld; and there was no man who sought after my soul;” that is, there was no man to imitate Mine example.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ in His Passion is the Speaker.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I looked on my right hand,” that is, not on the ungodly Jews, but on Mine own right hand, the Apostles,—“and there was no man who sought after My soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So thoroughly was there no man to “seek after My soul,” that he who had presumed on his own strength, “denied My soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because a man’s soul is sought after in two ways, either in order that you may enjoy his society; or that you may persecute him; therefore he here speaks of others, whom he would have “confounded and ashamed,” who are “seeking after his soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But lest you should understand it in the same way as when he complains of some who did not “seek after his soul,” He adds, “to destroy it;” that is, they seek after my soul in order to my death.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be turned backward and put to shame that wish me evil.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turned backwards.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not take this in a bad sense.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He wishes them well; and it is His voice, who said from the Cross, “Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore then doth he say to them, that they should return “backwards”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they who before were proud, so that they fell, are now become humble, so that they may rise again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when they are before, they are wishing to take precedence of their Lord; to be better than He; but if they go behind Him, they acknowledge Him to be better than they; they acknowledge that He ought to go before; that He should precede, they follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence He thus rebukes Peter giving Him evil counsel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord, when about to suffer for our salvation, also foretold what was to happen concerning that Passion itself; and Peter says, “Be it far from Thee,” “God forbid it!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This shall not be!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He would fain have gone before his Lord; would have given counsel to his Master!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Lord, that He might make him not go before Him, but follow after Him, says, “Get thee behind, Satan!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is for this reason He said “Satan,” because thou art seeking to go before Him, whom thou oughtest to follow; but if thou art behind, if thou follow Him, thou wilt henceforth not be “Satan.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Upon this Rock I will build My Church.” …

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them speedily bear away their own confusion, that say unto me, Well done!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Well done!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They praise you without reason.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A great man!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A man of education and of learning; but why a Christian?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They praise those things in you which you should wish not to be praised; they find fault with that at which you rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if perhaps you say, “What is it you praise in me, O man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That I am a virtuous man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If you think this, Christ made me this; praise Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the other says, “Be it far from you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do yourself no wrong!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You yourself made yourself such.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be confounded who say unto me, Well done!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what follows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let all those that seek Thee, O Lord, rejoice and be glad” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who “seek” not me, but “Thee;” who say not to me, “Well done!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Well done!” but see me “glory in Thee,” if I have anything whereof to glory; for “he who glories, let him glory in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let all those who seek Thee, Lord, rejoice and be glad.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And say continually, the Lord be magnified.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even if the sinner becometh righteous, thou shouldest give the glory to “Him who justifieth the ungodly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whether therefore it be a sinner, let Him be praised who calls him to forgiveness; or one already walking in the way of righteousness, let Him be praised who calls him to receive the crown!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Name of the Lord be magnified continually by “such as love Thy salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I for whom they were seeking evil, I whose “life they were seeking, that they might take it away.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But turn thee to another description of persons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I to whom they said, “Well done!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am poor and needy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is nothing in me that may be praised as mine own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him rend my sackcloth in sunder, and cover me with His robe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “Now I live, not I myself; but Christ liveth in me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If it is Christ that “liveth in thee,” and all that thou hast is Christ’s, and all that thou art to have hereafter is Christ’s also; what art thou in thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am poor and needy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now I am not rich, because I am not proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was rich who said, “Lord, I thank Thee that I am not as other men are;” but the publican was poor, who said, “Lord, be merciful to me a sinner!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The one was belching from his fulness; the other from want was crying piteously, “I am poor and needy!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what wouldest thou do, O poor and needy man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Beg at God’s door; “Knock, and it shall be opened unto thee.”—“As for me, I am poor and needy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet the Lord careth for me.”—“Cast thy care upon the Lord, and He shall bring it to pass.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What canst thou effect for thyself by taking care what canst thou provide for thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him who made thee “care for thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who cared for thee before thou wert, how shall He fail to have a care of thee, now that thou art what He would have thee be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now thou art a believer, now thou art walking in the “way of righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall not He have a care for thee, who “maketh His sun rise on the good and on the evil, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust”? …

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art my Help, and my Deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is calling upon God, imploring Him, fearing lest he should fall away: “Make no tarrying.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by “make no tarrying”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We lately read concerning the days of tribulation: “Unless those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The members of Christ—the Body of Christ extended everywhere—are asking of God, as one single person, one single poor man, and beggar!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He too was poor, who “though He was rich, yet became poor, that ye through His poverty might be made rich.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is He that maketh rich those who are the true poor; and maketh poor those who are falsely rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He crieth unto Him; “From the end of the earth I cried unto Thee, when my heart was in heaviness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There will come days of tribulations, and of greater tribulations; they will come even as the Scripture speaks: and as days advance, so are tribulations increased also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one promise himself what the Gospel doth not promise.…
